fresh_facts.claim: Iran's IRGC seized two ships (MSC Francesca and Epimeonis) in the Strait of Hormuz early Wednesday, April 22, 2026, and fired on a third vessel (Euphoria) which escaped to UAE.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service report with video footage

fresh_facts.claim: The US maintains a naval blockade on Iranian ports, estimated to cost Iran $500 million daily according to US assessments.
US forces seized a second Iran-linked oil tanker, Majestic X, in the Indian Ocean, targeting IRGC smuggling networks with Pentagon footage documenting boarding operations.
Three US aircraft carrier strike groups are positioned in the region as of April 23, 2026, providing military options according to US intelligence.
A US ceasefire extension was announced on April 22, 2026, with Iran's ceasefire originally set to expire April 22.
Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf supports US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad while IRGC Commander Major General Ahmad Vahidi opposes them, indicating leadership division.
IRGC Commander Ahmad Vahidi has direct access to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, giving him significant influence over negotiations according to reporting.
Iranian officials reported inability to contact Supreme Leader Khamenei, hindering negotiation progress as of April 19, 2026.
Divisions within Iranian leadership prevented a full Iranian team from attending Islamabad peace talks.
US President Trump stated the US controls the Strait of Hormuz and cited divisions between Iranian 'hardliners' and 'moderates' in leadership.
Pakistan-mediated peace talks between the US and Iran collapsed as of April 23, 2026.
Iran rejected return to negotiations under US terms including uranium transfer requirements.
Iran filed UN complaints accusing the US of using Qatar, Kuwait, UAE, and Saudi bases and airspace for strikes.
Iran linked one seized ship to Israel and tightened control of the Strait of Hormuz, refusing to reopen it while under US blockade.
Hezbollah (Iran-backed) launched rockets at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon as of April 23, 2026.
Iran lists retaliation targets focused on energy routes if US-Israeli strikes resume.
Iran retains thousands of missiles and drones threatening US and Israeli forces according to US assessments.
IRGC mapped undersea cables as a warning signal amid economic and military tensions as of April 23, 2026.
Iran experienced the most serious protest unrest ever documented as of February 3, 2026, prompting brutal crackdown and internet blackouts.
Supreme Leader Khamenei issued 'shoot to kill' orders against protesters on January 9, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: ISW assessment citing Iranian government sources

fresh_facts.claim: Iran deployed approximately 800-5,000 Iranian-backed Iraqi militias for internal repression as of February 3, 2026.
Iranian security forces made arrests of military members refusing to fire on protesters as of February 3, 2026.
IRGC documented concerns over potential defections among personnel as of February 3, 2026.
Iran requested foreign aid from Russia and China to address internal unrest as of January 2026.
Iran's regime framed protesters as 'terrorists' in official communications as of February 3, 2026.
IRGC confiscated Starlink devices during crackdowns on protesters as of February 3, 2026.
Iranian officials demonstrated high security force casualties and asset movement abroad as indicators of regime instability as of February 3, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran-US escalation intensifies as ceasefire collapses: IRGC seized multiple commercial vessels and reimposed Strait of Hormuz blockade while US enforces comprehensive naval/economic blockade costing Iran $500M daily, three carrier strike groups positioned for combat, and Pakistan-mediated peace talks have collapsed with Iran rejecting US negotiation terms.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Pakistan-mediated US-Iran peace talks collapsed on April 23, 2026, as Iran rejected US terms including uranium transfer requirements; simultaneously, Pakistan-US ceasefire extension was announced on April 22 but Iran's IRGC seized MSC Francesca and Epimeonis in the Strait of Hormuz, fired on Euphoria, and Iran filed UN complaints alleging US strikes from regional ally bases.
